summary: Design a deep reinforcement learning algorithm capable of incrementally expanding its action space as new actions become available (e.g., new abilities unlocked in video game levels), without retraining from scratch or catastrophically forgetting previously learned action-value mappings.
assumptions: Base algorithm is DQN (Mnih et al. 2015) extended with a progressive network or adapter-based architecture for the output layer.
New actions arrive in discrete batches at known or detectable transition points, and can be mapped to embedding representations for better generalization.
Experience replay buffers store only transitions executed under the current action set; retroactive experience for new actions is not available.
The existing policy for old actions must remain functional after each expansion (no catastrophic forgetting).
